name: novelbits-logscope
description: Real-time embedded log viewer with Bluetooth LE HCI decoding — Zephyr logs with filtering, search, fault detection, and Wireshark export

enumDescriptions: J-Link RTT via pylink (recommended — works with any J-Link device)
Connect to J-Link RTT telnet server (legacy)
logscope.transport.description: Transport mode for reading RTT data.

logscope.rtt.address.description: RTT control block address (hex, e.g. 0x20004050). Set to 'auto' to detect from Zephyr ELF automatically.

logscope.rtt.pollInterval.description: RTT poll interval in milliseconds (nrfutil transport only). Lower = faster updates but more SWD traffic.

logscope.nrfutil.path.description: Path to nrfutil binary. Leave as 'nrfutil' if it's on your PATH.

logscope.rtt.host.description: J-Link RTT telnet server host (jlink-telnet transport only)

logscope.rtt.port.description: J-Link RTT telnet server port (jlink-telnet transport only)

logscope.maxEntries.description: Maximum number of log entries to keep in memory

logscope.autoConnect.description: Automatically connect to the last-used device when LogScope opens.

logscope.lastDevice.description: Last connected device (used for auto-connect). Set automatically.

logscope.logWrap.description: Wrap long log messages instead of truncating. Toggle via the wrap button in the viewer.

logscope.jlink.path.description: Path to JLinkExe. Leave empty to auto-detect.

logscope.jlink.device.description: Target device name for J-Link connection (use Cortex-M33 for nRF54L15)

logscope.jlink.autoStart.description: Automatically start J-Link when connecting. Disable to connect to an existing RTT server.

logscope.jlink.rttSearchRanges.description: RTT control block search range (base size in hex). Default covers 512KB starting at 0x20000000 which works for most ARM Cortex-M devices.
